_provenance.stage: Stage 1 — derived from live cards for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
_provenance.note: This Stage 1 JSON was reconstructed from the currently-published cards so external Stage 2 (Gemini bias) and Stage 3 (Codex fact-check) reviews can verify what readers see. It is not the original analysis — the original pipeline was not run on this issue.

headline: Vegetable Prices May Jump 50% in April: The Fertiliser Link Nobody Explains
context: One-third of global fertiliser shipments transit the Strait of Hormuz. With the waterway blocked since February 2026, fertiliser raw materials surged 100-150%. Hari Raya price controls on 12 vegetables ended 28 March. Farmers warn 30-50% price hikes in April.

cards.text: Food prices are rising because of the Middle East conflict. That is the convenient headline.
cards.sub: The chain runs from Hormuz through fertiliser ships to Cameron Highlands to your pasar; it also runs through decades of policy that left Malaysia with no stockpile and no domestic production.

cards.h: 23 fertiliser vessels blocked at Hormuz. Nitrogen fertiliser jumped from RM240 to RM305/25kg in two weeks, projected RM350 by mid-April.
cards.s: Wholesaler margins already extract 30-50%. A farmer sells kangkung at 30 sen/kg; you pay RM3. The supply chain was broken before any strait closed.
cards.d: 23 fertiliser vessels blocked at Hormuz. Nitrogen fertiliser jumped from RM240 to RM305/25kg in two weeks, projected RM350 by mid-April. Wholesaler margins already extract 30-50%. A farmer sells kangkung at 30 sen/kg; you pay RM3. The supply chain was broken before any strait closed.

cards.h: B40 spend 40%+ of income on food; T20 about 15%. A 50% vegetable hike is a rounding error for one group, a skipped meal for the other.
cards.s: Women manage 80% of household food purchases, absorbing shocks through smaller portions, fewer meals. Invisible in inflation stats, visible in child nutrition.
cards.d: B40 spend 40%+ of income on food; T20 about 15%. A 50% vegetable hike is a rounding error for one group, a skipped meal for the other. Women manage 80% of household food purchases, absorbing shocks through smaller portions, fewer meals. Invisible in inflation stats, visible in child nutrition.

cards.h: Indonesia stockpiles fertiliser via Pupuk Indonesia; Vietnam's price law covers fertilisers. Malaysia has neither.
cards.s: Thailand declared fertiliser a controlled good with monitored reserves. Malaysia imports nearly all fertiliser, holds no strategic stock and has no stabilisation framework.
cards.d: Indonesia stockpiles fertiliser via Pupuk Indonesia; Vietnam's price law covers fertilisers. Malaysia has neither. Thailand declared fertiliser a controlled good with monitored reserves. Malaysia imports nearly all fertiliser, holds no strategic stock and has no stabilisation framework.

cards.text: The question is not why prices are rising. It is why a country that knew Hormuz was a chokepoint for fertiliser never stockpiled a single bag.
Hormuz exposed the fragility, but home built it: palm-oil priorities, no fertiliser reserves, middlemen taking more than farmers. War made it visible; policy made it inevitable.

confidence: Low — derived from published cards, original brief not on disk; submit for retroactive Stage 2/3 verification
